reviewer: solo new-family build 2026-07-21 — NLI correctness proven FIRST in headless Chrome (entailment 0.98 / contradiction 1.00 / neutral 1.00 on known pairs; Eiffel-Paris vs Eiffel-Berlin -> contradiction), then the deployed page driven end-to-end (Entailment/Contradiction/Neutral example chips flip the verdict). Text-only demo. Distinct from the 4 built zero-shot-classification (label) demos.

rubric.evidence: RUNNABILITY + CORRECTNESS verified in headless Chrome (transformers.js 3.7.5, WASM, q8) BEFORE building. On known premise/hypothesis pairs the model gives the right 3-way relation: 'A man is playing a guitar on stage.' / 'A person is making music.' -> entailment 0.98; / 'The man is asleep in bed.' -> contradiction 1.00; / 'The man is a famous musician.' -> neutral 1.00; 'Eiffel Tower is in Paris.' / 'Eiffel Tower is in Berlin.' -> contradiction 1.00. Deployed page (scripts/validate-nli.mjs 8/8): the three example chips flip the verdict correctly. Real reasoning, not canned.
rubric.notes: Inference runs off the main thread in a module Worker via AutoTokenizer + AutoModelForSequenceClassification; a q8 xsmall forward pass is well under a second.

rubric.evidence: A sentence PAIR (premise, hypothesis) -> logits[3] -> softmax over entailment/neutral/contradiction. A DISTINCT capability: sentence-pair textual entailment, vs the built zero-shot-classification demos which run an NLI model with a fixed 'This example is {label}' template to label ONE text, and vs single-text classifiers (sentiment/emotion/etc.).
rubric.notes: The hypothesis being a free sentence (not a label template) is exactly what surfaces the fact-checking / RAG-grounding use case.

rubric.evidence: At-a-glance declares the model (nli-deberta-v3-xsmall cross-encoder, linked), task (text-classification over a pair / NLI), the pair-tokenization detail (tokenizer(premise, {text_pair: hypothesis}), run low-level because the pipeline ignores the pair), input/output, quantisation (q8), download (~91 MB) and license (Apache-2.0). No invented API.
rubric.notes: The 'why low-level' note is important + honest: the convenience pipeline silently drops the second sentence, so the demo tokenizes the pair explicitly.

rubric.evidence: Loads through the shared createModelLoader auto-init architecture (model {modelId:'onnx-community/nli-deberta-v3-xsmall-ONNX', runtime:'transformers.js', dtype:'q8', sizeMB:91}). transformers.js caches the model files, so lib/model-cache.js sees the cached model-id path -> a returning visit auto-initialises with NO Download click; first visit shows an explicit Download (~91 MB). Verified: fresh profile showed Download, clicking it flipped to ready and inferred the first example.
rubric.notes: Standard transformers.js runtime-owned whole-file caching, adopted via the shared loader + <model-download-status>.

rubric.evidence: checking -> Download (absent) -> ready (auto-infers the Entailment example) -> debounced live re-infer on either input + example chips; a busy/queued guard coalesces rapid edits; error path via the shared loader + a per-run try/catch surfaced in the status line. Verified download -> ready -> entailment -> contradiction -> neutral end-to-end.
rubric.notes: Empty premise or hypothesis clears the verdict. 500 ms debounce keeps live typing smooth.

rubric.evidence: Two editable sentence inputs (premise + hypothesis) plus five example chips spanning the three relations AND the applied use cases (Fact-check: water boils at 50C -> contradiction; RAG grounding: a specific figure vs a vaguer claim -> entailment). All drive a live 3-way result.
rubric.notes: The RAG-grounding + fact-check chips make the distinct value obvious vs a plain label classifier.

rubric.evidence: The verdict + all three bars are the model's real softmax probabilities; the winning class is argmax and its plain-language meaning is shown. Ambiguous pairs land on neutral with split probabilities rather than a forced call. Text-only demo, so no media provenance concerns.
rubric.notes: Honest that it's a small (xsmall) cross-encoder — good on clear pairs, and the bars expose uncertainty on subtle ones.

rubric.evidence: The verdict is role=status aria-live=polite; both inputs are labelled; example chips are native buttons; each bar row shows the label name + percentage (text), so the relation isn't conveyed by colour alone.
rubric.notes: Colours (green/amber/red) reinforce the relation but the label word + verdict sentence carry it for non-colour users.

rubric.evidence: Full-width inputs (box-sizing: border-box), flex-wrap chips, and a capped-width bar column. Verified 0 horizontal overflow at desktop AND mobile 360px (validate-nli.mjs), 0 console errors.
rubric.notes: Text-only layout; verified desktop+mobile. Subpage ladder is a recorded follow-up.

rubric.evidence: Inference runs in a dedicated module Worker; a q8 deberta-v3-xsmall pair forward pass is sub-second, and a 500 ms debounce + busy/queued coalescing keep live editing smooth. The ~91 MB q8 download is a one-time cost.
rubric.notes: Single pair per request, so cost is flat regardless of how many example chips are clicked.

guidanceConsulted.query: run a transformers.js sequence-pair classifier off the main thread; keep two live inputs responsive
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Move inference off the main thread; keep interactions responsive (INP); debounce live input; coalesce rapid edits.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inference runs in a module Worker; both inputs are debounced 500 ms with a busy/queued guard.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: sub-second per pair; page responsive while editing premise + hypothesis

guidanceConsulted.query: full-width text inputs + a compact result that never overflows horizontally
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Use box-sizing: border-box for full-width inputs; flex-wrap chips; cap the result width.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — inputs are width:100% box-sizing:border-box; chips flex-wrap; the bar column is max-width capped.

guidanceConsulted.query: announce a changing verdict to assistive tech; label the two inputs; avoid colour-only results
guidanceConsulted.recommendation: Announce the live verdict via role=status aria-live; label inputs; show the relation as text, not colour alone.
guidanceConsulted.appliedOrException: applied — the verdict is role=status aria-live=polite; both inputs are labelled; each bar shows its label + percentage.
guidanceConsulted.evidence: the verdict + plain-language meaning are announced on change

openQuestions: Would a batch mode (one premise vs many hypotheses) better demonstrate the RAG-grounding use case than single pairs?
Is xsmall accurate enough for real fact-checking, or should the page offer a larger NLI variant as an option?
followUpGoals: Add a RAG-grounding batch mode: paste an answer + several retrieved snippets, and rank which snippets support it.
Offer a larger NLI model (base/large) as a higher-accuracy option for harder pairs.
Build a basics/practical/wild subpage ladder (clear textbook pairs; real claim-vs-evidence; adversarial near-paraphrases where entailment is genuinely hard).
summary: Textual entailment / NLI (nli-deberta-v3-xsmall) — a distinct sentence-pair capability (premise + hypothesis -> entailment/neutral/contradiction), separate from the built zero-shot-classification label demos, proven correct in the browser on transformers.js WASM (entailment 0.98, contradiction 1.00, fact-check Eiffel-Berlin -> contradiction). Tokenized as a pair (the pipeline drops the second sentence). ~91 MB q8, sub-second. Index demo complete + verified 8/8; a RAG-grounding batch mode + a larger variant + subpage ladder are recorded follow-ups.
